audit_status.source_base: Greek Gospel text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for Sabbath healing, hardness of heart, Pharisees/Herodians plot, crowds, appointment of the Twelve, Beelzebul controversy, binding the strong one, Spirit warning, and family redefinition around doing the Divine will.

verses.restored: He entered again into the synagogue, and a man with a withered hand was there.
They were watching him closely, whether he would heal him on the Sabbath, so that they might accuse him.
He said to the man with the withered hand, Rise into the middle.
He said to them, Is it lawful on the Sabbath to do good or to do harm, to save life/soul or to kill? But they were silent.
Looking around at them with anger, grieved at the hardness of their heart, he said to the man, Stretch out your hand. He stretched it out, and his hand was restored.
The Pharisees went out and immediately took counsel with the Herodians against him, how they might destroy him.
Jesus withdrew with his disciples to the sea, and a great multitude from Galilee followed, and from Judea,
from Jerusalem, Idumea, beyond the Jordan, and around Tyre and Sidon, a great multitude, hearing all he was doing, came to him.
He told his disciples that a little boat should be ready for him because of the crowd, so they would not crush him.
For he healed many, so that all who had afflictions pressed upon him to touch him.
Whenever the unclean spirits saw him, they fell before him and cried out, saying, You are the Son of the Divine.
He strictly warned them many times that they should not make him known.
He went up into the mountain and called those whom he wanted, and they came to him.
He appointed twelve, whom he also named apostles/sent ones, so that they might be with him and so that he might send them to proclaim,
and to have authority to cast out unclean spirits.
He appointed the twelve: Simon, to whom he gave the name Peter;
James son of Zebedee and John the brother of James, to whom he gave the name Boanerges, that is, Sons of Thunder;
Andrew, Philip, Bartholomew, Matthew, Thomas, James son of Alphaeus, Thaddaeus, Simon the Cananaean/Zealot,
and Judas Iscariot, who also handed him over. Then he came into a house.
The crowd gathered again, so that they were not even able to eat bread.
When those near him heard, they went out to seize him, for they were saying, He is beside himself.
The scribes who came down from Jerusalem were saying, He has Beelzebul, and, By the ruler of the unclean spirits he casts out the spirits.
Calling them to him, he spoke to them in parables: How can Satan/adversary cast out Satan/adversary?
If a reign is divided against itself, that reign is not able to stand.
If a house is divided against itself, that house will not be able to stand.
If the adversary has risen against himself and is divided, he is not able to stand but has an end.
But no one is able to enter the strong one's house and plunder his goods unless first he binds the strong one; then he will plunder his house.
Amen, I say to you: all things will be released for the children of humanity, the sins/misalignments and whatever blasphemies they speak.
But whoever blasphemes against the holy Spirit does not have release into the age, but is held by age-long sin/misalignment.
because they were saying, He has an unclean spirit.
His mother and his brothers came, and standing outside they sent to him, calling him.
A crowd was sitting around him, and they said to him, Look, your mother and your brothers and sisters are outside seeking you.
He answered them, Who are my mother and my brothers?
Looking around at those sitting in a circle around him, he said, Look, my mother and my brothers.
Whoever does the will of the Divine, this one is my brother and sister and mother.
